name: Thane Eugene Cesar
description: Thane Eugene Cesar is a figure primarily associated with conspiracy theories surrounding the assassination of Robert F. Kennedy. Proponents claim that Cesar was deeply involved in the assassination, potentially as a second shooter or part of a larger conspiracy involving the CIA or other government entities. Cesar, a security guard at the Ambassador Hotel on the night of the assassination, is alleged to have been a suspect who was never adequately investigated by law enforcement. The theory often centers on purported inconsistencies in Cesar's statements, witness testimonies, and physical evidence. Some researchers suggest that Cesar's background and connections remain shrouded in secrecy, fueling suspicions of a cover-up. However, mainstream investigations, including those conducted by law enforcement and independent researchers, have largely concluded that Sirhan Sirhan acted alone, and that Cesar's involvement, if any, was minimal and does not support the existence of a broader conspiracy. Critics argue that the claims against Cesar are based on circumstantial evidence, misinterpretations of witness accounts, and a selective focus on details that support the conspiracy narrative while ignoring contradictory evidence. Mainstream media and academic historians generally dismiss the conspiracy theories surrounding Cesar as lacking credible evidence and relying on speculative interpretations of events.
subtitle: Alleged RFK assassination conspirator.

claims.claim: Thane Eugene Cesar was a second shooter in the RFK assassination.
claims.evidence: Witness accounts describing a second shooter, discrepancies in bullet trajectories, and Cesar's presence at the scene.
claims.criticism: Most witness accounts support Sirhan Sirhan as the sole shooter. Bullet trajectory analysis is contested, and Cesar's presence as a security guard is explained.

claims.claim: Cesar was connected to the CIA or other government agencies and involved in a conspiracy to assassinate RFK.
claims.evidence: Allegations of Cesar's prior military service and potential links to intelligence communities.
claims.criticism: No credible evidence has been found to support these allegations. Cesar's military service, if any, does not automatically imply involvement in a conspiracy.

claims.claim: The official investigation into the RFK assassination was a cover-up, protecting Cesar and other potential conspirators.
claims.evidence: Alleged suppression of evidence, inadequate investigation of Cesar, and inconsistencies in the official narrative.
claims.criticism: Multiple investigations have been conducted, and while some inconsistencies exist, they do not definitively prove a cover-up or Cesar's involvement in a conspiracy.

keyFigures.name: Lisa Pease
keyFigures.role: Researcher and proponent of the second shooter theory involving Cesar.

keyFigures.name: Dan Moldea
keyFigures.role: Investigative journalist who has examined the RFK assassination and Cesar's role, but remains skeptical of the second shooter theory.

keyFigures.name: Vincent Bugliosi
keyFigures.role: Prosecutor and author of 'Reclaiming History: The Assassination of President John F. Kennedy', who argued that Sirhan Sirhan acted alone.

timeline.event: Robert F. Kennedy is assassinated at the Ambassador Hotel in Los Angeles.
Conspiracy theories surrounding the assassination and Thane Eugene Cesar begin to emerge.
Independent investigations and documentaries explore the assassination, some focusing on Cesar's alleged involvement.

sources.author: Lisa Pease
sources.work: Various articles and essays on the RFK assassination.
sources.title: Lisa Pease
